message:
fix(libs): harden sourced-context behavior and namespace script-internal globals

Seven ACFS shell scripts (`onboard`, `cheatsheet`, `dashboard`,
`export-config`, `info`, `status`, `support`) are routinely sourced by
other scripts to reuse their resolver helpers (`*_home_for_user`,
`*_prepare_context`, redaction/logging). The as-merged versions had
two bugs in that mode:

**Bug 1 — caller-env pollution**
Each script `set -euo pipefail` at the top and never restored shell
options, so a caller that wasn't using errexit/nounset/pipefail
would suddenly have them on after the source call — and any later
unset var would kill a perfectly fine parent script with a hard exit
under `-u`. They also assigned raw globals like `SCRIPT_PATH`,
`SCRIPT_DIR`, and `ACFS_HOME` directly into the caller's namespace,
clobbering whatever the caller had set there.

Fix: capture `_{NAME}_RESTORE_ERREXIT/NOUNSET/PIPEFAIL`,
`_{NAME}_WAS_SOURCED`, and `_{NAME}_ORIGINAL_HOME*` at top-of-file
(before the `set -e...`); add a `{name}_restore_shell_options_if_sourced`
function that runs at script-end and is a no-op when executed
directly; wrap `main "$@"` in `if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]`
so sourcing never invokes the CLI entry point. Rename exposed
globals to underscore-prefixed script-local variants:
`SCRIPT_PATH` → `_ONBOARD_SCRIPT_PATH`,
`SCRIPT_DIR` → `_ONBOARD_SCRIPT_DIR`,
`ACFS_HOME` → `_{NAME}_ACFS_HOME`.

**Bug 2 — stale installed state shadowing system state**
`*_resolve_runtime_home` and `*_prepare_context` consulted the
per-install `ACFS_HOME/state.json` BEFORE `/var/lib/acfs/state.json`
for both `target_user` and `target_home`. That meant a repo-local
ACFS with a leftover `state.json` from an earlier run (or, in tests,
an intentionally poisoned one) would always win over the
system-maintained state file — exactly the scenario commit 4d19ebde
caught for `target_home` in cheatsheet+dashboard only. Extend the
precedence flip to:
- `target_user` resolution (system state first, install state second),
  in `cheatsheet`, `dashboard`, `status`, `export-config`, `info`,
  `support`, and `onboard`;
- `target_home` resolution in `onboard`, `info`, `status`, `support`,
  `export-config` (catching the scripts 4d19ebde missed).

**Tests**
14 new cases in `tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh`, two per
script:
- `test_{name}_can_be_sourced_without_{running_main | mutating_caller_env |
  mutating_caller_home}`: sources the lib from a caller with known
  HOME / shell options / no intent to run main, verifies the caller
  state is identical after the source returns.
- `test_{name}_repo_local_prefers_system_state_target_user_over_stale_installed_state`:
  poisons the installed `state.json` via the new
  `poison_installed_target_user` helper, writes a distinct
  `target_user` to the system state, asserts the script resolves to
  the system value.

Also: `tests/test_support_redaction.sh` now sources `support.sh` directly
(now safe, post-fix) instead of extracting function bodies via awk
— simpler and exercises the sourcing path that the new
`test_support_can_be_sourced_without_running_main` case relies on.
The `run.log` `printf` call in `test_changelog_export_status.sh` is
collapsed onto one line so the escape-free `
` inside the format
string stops looking like a heredoc to a quick reader.

**shellcheck notes updated**
SC2034/SC2317 disables in `test_support_redaction.sh` replaced the
"eval'd functions" rationale (obsolete once we `source` directly)
with the current truth: YELLOW is part of the shared palette pattern,
and `log_*` stubs are fallbacks when support.sh is sourced without
logging helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
